PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED THAT THE NEGLIGENT OR WRONGFUL CONDUCT OF DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S EMPLOYEE ACTING WITHIN THE SCOPE OF HIS OR HER EMPLOYMENT CREATED THE DANGEROUS CONDITION OR THAT IT HAD NOTICE OF THE DANGEROUS CONDITION FOR A LONG ENOUGH TIME TO HAVE PROTECTED AGAINST IT" (GOV. CODE 835.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DIANE P. CRAGG, ESQ., 415.524.4300, FOR DEFENDANTS GEORGE HERBST AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not alleged that the negligent or wrongful conduct of Defendant City of San Francisco's employee acting within the scope of his or her employment created the dangerous condition or that it had notice of the dangerous condition for a long enough time to have protected against it" (Gov. Code 835.) Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
